Class Grid22

java.lang.Object
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.Grid
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.Grid22
public class Grid22 extends Grid
A concrete Grid that provides the initial puzzle state for Q22 ("Shrek").

This class defines the starting configuration for the 2018 PDC Q22 puzzle. Its sole purpose is to load a pre-computed bitmask representing the initial layout of true cells into the Grid.gridState array.

Architectural Role

As a concrete implementation of the abstract Grid, this class represents one of the specific problems the solver is designed to tackle. The initial state is loaded via the initialize() method, which uses a hardcoded bitmask for maximum performance, avoiding any computational overhead at runtime.

Puzzle Details

The Q22 grid is initialized with 50 true cells. A known 15-click solution is included in the source code, which can be uncommented for validation or testing of the solver's logic.

Since:
2025.03 - Concrete Class Introduction
See Also:
  • Grid13
  • Grid35
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) for initialization and most operations.
Threading Model:
This class is not thread-safe. Each thread should use its own instance of Grid22 to avoid concurrency issues.
Memory Management:
No allocations after initialization (except for methods that explicitly create new objects).

      public Grid22()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Loads the pre-computed state for the Q22 puzzle.

      This method directly assigns the bitmask representing the puzzle's initial state to the Grid.gridState array. It also sets cached values for the first true cell and the total count of true cells for efficient processing.

      The commented-out clicks represent the known 15-click solution and can be used for testing or validation purposes.

      Specified by:
      initialize in class Grid
      Since:
      2025.03 - Concrete Class Introduction
      See Also:
      • Grid.getGridState()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) - Direct assignment of pre-computed values.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; mutates instance state.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
